# wl-device-kit

<!--
Copyright (C) 2023 Work Louder Inc. - All Rights Reserved
Unauthorized copying of this file, via any medium is strictly prohibited
Proprietary and confidential

Written by Cristian Duciuc <cristian@worklouder.cc>, March 2026
-->

Node.js library for discovering, connecting to, and communicating with Work Louder hardware (keyboards, macro pads, and knobs) over USB HID and serial.

The package root export (`@worklouder/wl-device-kit`) is Node-oriented and includes discovery/transport modules.

## Browser-safe enums (renderer)

For Electron renderer or browser contexts that only need shared enums/types, import from the `browser_safe` subpath:

```ts
import { DeviceType, DeviceLayoutType } from "@worklouder/wl-device-kit/browser_safe";
```

## Requirements

- Node.js >= 18
- Yarn 1.x (classic)

## Install

This is a private package hosted on [GitHub Packages](https://github.com/worklouder/wl-device-kit/packages). Authenticate with a GitHub personal access token that has `read:packages` scope, then install:

```bash
# .npmrc (project or user level)
@worklouder:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${GITHUB_TOKEN}
```

```bash
yarn add @worklouder/wl-device-kit
```

## Build

```bash
yarn install
yarn build     # compiles TypeScript to dist/
```

## Architecture

```
src/
├── common/types/       # Shared enums and interfaces (DeviceType, ConnectionType, WLDevice, JSON-RPC types)
├── utils/              # EventEmitter, string escaping helpers
├── wl_device_discovery/# USB HID + serial port scanning
├── wl_device_comm/     # Low-level transport layer (HID & serial framing, request queue)
├── wl_rpc_client/      # JSON-RPC 2.0 request/response serialization
└── wl_rpc_api/         # High-level typed API (system, filesystem, lighting, media, UI)
```

| Module | Entry class | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Device Discovery** | `WLDeviceDiscovery` | Scan USB for connected Work Louder devices and bootloaders |
| **Device Comm** | `WLDeviceCommImpl` | Open a transport connection (HID or serial), manage the send queue |
| **RPC Client** | `WLRPCClient` | Serialize JSON-RPC 2.0 requests, manage call IDs, parse responses |
| **RPC API** | `WLRPCApi` | High-level typed methods for every firmware capability |

## Quick start

```ts
import {
  WLDeviceDiscovery,
  WLDeviceCommImpl,
  WLRPCApi,
  ConnectionEventType,
} from "@worklouder/wl-device-kit";

// 1. Discover devices
const discovery = new WLDeviceDiscovery();
const devices = discovery.findWLDevices();

if (devices.length === 0) {
  console.log("No devices found");
  process.exit(1);
}

// 2. Connect
const comm = new WLDeviceCommImpl();

comm.onConnectionEvent((event) => {
  if (event.type === ConnectionEventType.DISCONNECTED) {
    console.log("Device disconnected");
  }
});

await comm.connect(devices[0]);

// 3. Use the high-level API
const api = new WLRPCApi(comm);

const status = await api.getDeviceStatus();
console.log("Firmware:", status.firmwareVersion);
console.log("Battery:", status.batteryPercentage, "%");

// 4. Disconnect when done
await comm.disconnect();
```

## Device discovery

```ts
const discovery = new WLDeviceDiscovery();

// All Work Louder HID devices
const all = discovery.findWLDevices();

// Filter by product type
import { DeviceType } from "@worklouder/wl-device-kit";
const knobs = discovery.findWLDevices([DeviceType.Knob]);

// Bootloader (DFU) devices on serial ports
const bootloaders = await discovery.findWLBootloaderDevices();
```

## Connection events

Subscribe to transport lifecycle changes on the `WLDeviceComm` instance:

```ts
const unsub = comm.onConnectionEvent((event) => {
  switch (event.type) {
    case ConnectionEventType.CONNECTED:
      console.log("Connected");
      break;
    case ConnectionEventType.DISCONNECTED:
      console.log("Disconnected");
      break;
    case ConnectionEventType.ERROR:
      console.error("Error:", event.error);
      break;
  }
});

// Stop listening
unsub();
```

## Device notifications

Devices can send unsolicited JSON-RPC notifications (no `id`, just a `method`). Register handlers on the comm layer:

```ts
comm.addNotifyHandler("kb.radial", (params) => {
  console.log("Radial menu event:", params);
});

// Remove when no longer needed
comm.removeNotifyHandler("kb.radial");
```

## Lighting

`WLDeviceLightingConfig` has two zones — `backlight` and `underglow` — each a `SideLightingConfig`:

```ts
import { LightingEffect } from "@worklouder/wl-device-kit";
import type { WLDeviceLightingConfig } from "@worklouder/wl-device-kit";

const config: WLDeviceLightingConfig = {
  backlight: {
    effect: LightingEffect.solid,
    brightness: 0.8,
    color: 0xFF5500, // packed RGB int32
  },
  underglow: {
    effect: LightingEffect.rainbow,
    speed: 0.5,
    brightness: 1.0,
  },
};

await api.sendLightingPreview(config);
```

> **Note:** `color` is a **packed RGB integer** (e.g. `0xFF0000` for red), not a hex string. `brightness`, `speed`, and `magic` are **normalized 0–1**.

## Custom logger

All constructors accept an optional `Logger` object. If omitted, logging is silently disabled (nothing is printed). Pass `console` or your own logger to enable output:

```ts
const logger = {
  info: (...args: any[]) => myLogger.info(...args),
  error: (...args: any[]) => myLogger.error(...args),
  debug: (...args: any[]) => myLogger.debug(...args),
  warn: (...args: any[]) => myLogger.warn(...args),
};

const discovery = new WLDeviceDiscovery(logger);
const comm = new WLDeviceCommImpl(logger);
const api = new WLRPCApi(comm, logger);
```
